assertion_results.text: The changelog identifies this as a patch version bump (v4.2.1)
assertion_results.evidence: Header reads '## [4.2.1]' with note 'Patch release — security fix.'

assertion_results.text: The security vulnerability is described with advisory language including severity level (HIGH) and CVSS score (8.1)
assertion_results.evidence: Security section includes 'Severity: HIGH | CVSS: 8.1' in a callout block

assertion_results.text: Affected versions (4.0.0 - 4.2.0) are explicitly stated so operators know if they need to upgrade
The changelog communicates upgrade urgency — words like 'urgent', 'immediately', 'critical', or 'recommended to upgrade' appear
assertion_results.evidence: The changelog describes the fix but does not include urgency language. No 'urgent', 'immediately', or 'recommended to upgrade' found.

assertion_results.text: The open redirect vulnerability description explains the attack vector (URL-encoded characters bypassing validation)
assertion_results.evidence: Description states 'malicious redirect_uri using URL-encoded characters to bypass validation, redirecting users to attacker-controlled sites'

assertion_results.text: The CSRF fix (state parameter check) is documented as a separate entry from the redirect fix
assertion_results.evidence: Two separate entries: 'fix open redirect in OAuth callback validation' and 'add state parameter CSRF check to OAuth flow'

assertion_results.text: A compare link is included for the diff between v4.2.0 and v4.2.1
The changelog does NOT include the test commit as a user-facing change (tests are internal)
assertion_results.evidence: The test commit 'add regression tests for redirect validation' appears as a bullet point under an 'Other' section.
